what: THE QUESTION REGISTER - every candidate conditional + its PROOF MARK. This is the probe's ask-list, not a fact table.
NOT_A_FACT_SOURCE: Nothing here is a fact about the CoA client. Every entry is a QUESTION until a probe verdict replaces its proof_mark. Do not cite reference/ as basis/.
why_allowed: Recall and secondary sources are inadmissible as a FACT source but admissible as a QUESTION source, because the probe adjudicates. A wrong candidate costs one probe row reading 'unsupported'. A wrong fact poisons the basis silently.
absence_proves_nothing: A conditional no wiki lists is UN-ASKED, not unsupported. Demonstrated mechanically: ['@pettarget'] witnessed in this client's own Lua and in NO wiki.

proof_marks.UNPROVEN: claimed by a secondary source; NO witness of any kind on this client. A question, nothing more.
proof_marks.SOURCE-CORROBORATED: the client's own Lua witnesses the token (a handler compares the parser's returned target to it). Handler-side only, and target tokens only - never flags. Still wants the probe.
proof_marks.PROVEN-TRUE: probe: known and currently true. [PROBE WRITES THIS]
proof_marks.PROVEN-FALSE: probe: known and currently false. [PROBE WRITES THIS]
proof_marks.UNSUPPORTED: probe: parser rejects both polarities. [PROBE WRITES THIS]
proof_marks.IGNORED: probe: both polarities true => no-op. [PROBE WRITES THIS]

two_mechanisms.why_this_matters: `@unit` and `[flag]` are NOT the same mechanism and do NOT share a proof method. Conflating them is the easiest way to write a confident wrong guide.

@unit / target=.mechanism: HYPOTHESIS: a pass-through STRING. SecureCmdOptionParse appears to return whatever follows @; support lives DOWNSTREAM in the handler's own special-casing (/cast checks target=='cursor' -> Custom_HandleTerrainClick) and in unit resolution.
@unit / target=.independent_support: wowpedia/Macro_conditionals states '@unitId : Replace with any valid unitId' and '`target=` is also an alias for `@`' - a RETAIL doc describing the same pass-through shape the CoA handler code implies. Two directions, one hypothesis. Still a hypothesis.
@unit / target=.consequence: the polarity matrix does NOT apply; there is likely no [no@cursor].
@unit / target=.probe_row_that_settles_it: SecureCmdOptionParse("[@banana] X") - if a nonsense unit returns target="banana", @ is pass-through and the vocabulary question does not apply to it at all.
@unit / target=.status: HYPOTHESIS - not asserted. One probe row settles it.

[flag].mechanism: actually EVALUATED by the C parser (combat, mod:shift, group, ...).
[flag].consequence: THIS is the vocabulary question, and what the polarity matrix is for.

two_mechanisms.name_collision_worth_knowing: [cursor] (a FLAG - 'what the cursor is holding', per wowwiki-archive) is NOT @cursor (a TARGET - Ascension's terrain-click). Same word, two mechanisms.

era_signal_legend.patch-dated: SOURCED - the wiki carries a {{Patch}} annotation (often with a Blizzard citation). EVIDENCE. If the patch is > 3.x it postdates the 3.3.5a base => present only if BACKPORTED.
era_signal_legend.archive-documented: in the wowwiki archive (content ~2010, WotLK/early-Cata) => expected present on a 3.3.5a base
era_signal_legend.retail-documented-only: retail wiki lists it, the ~2010 archive does not, and NO patch annotation exists => UNDATED. We do NOT know when it shipped. Absence is not evidence.
era_signal_legend.ascension-claimed: only the Ascension wiki says so
era_signal_legend.NOT-IN-ANY-WIKI: only this client's own source says so

era_method_correction: era_signal originally called this bucket 'post-wotlk', inferring the history from ABSENCE in the ~2010 archive - the exact 'absence proves nothing' error this slice writes into three files, committed by the tool built to prevent it, in the field the probe steers by. It was RIGHT for the 4 conditionals carrying patch annotations - by LUCK, not method. Now: a patch annotation is evidence and dates the row; absence yields only 'retail-documented-only' = UNDATED.

ascension-wiki-macros.standing: SECONDARY - the server's own guide; inherits its conditional list from the retail wikis below (see its Resources section)

wowwiki-archive-making-a-macro.standing: SECONDARY (retail) - frozen 2018, documents spell RANKS => WotLK-era; closest external match to a 3.3.5a client. Carries a COMPLETENESS CLAIM for ITS era, which is not a claim about CoA.

wowpedia-making-a-macro.standing: SECONDARY (retail) - cited by the Ascension wiki; delegates its conditional list to Macro_conditionals

wowpedia-macro-conditionals.standing: SECONDARY (retail) - the delegation target of the cited page

grammar_claims.claim: clauses evaluate in order; the FIRST true one wins
`@unit`/`target=` is resolved FIRST, before the other conditionals
`@unit` also sets the unit the OTHER conditionals are checked against
`@unit`/`target=` is resolved FIRST, before the other conditionals
`@unitId` takes ANY valid unitId => supports the PASS-THROUGH hypothesis

claimed_by.says: Immediately targets the ground under the cursor.

@cursor.era_note: SOURCED: added in patch 7.1.0 (Legion). The CoA client is 3.3.5a (WotLK), so this POSTDATES its base => present here only if BACKPORTED. This client backports freely, and @cursor (7.1.0) is a CONFIRMED backport witnessed in its own source - so presence is a real question, not a formality.
